workflow.name: Predict no-show risk and route reminders with Google Calendar, Sheets, Slack and OpenAI
workflow.description:
## Who is this for
Business owners and service providers who want to reduce no-show rates for appointments booked via Google Calendar.

## What this workflow does
This workflow fetches tomorrow's Google Calendar bookings, looks up each customer's no-show history from Google Sheets, and calculates a risk score (0-100) from four weighted signals. Based on the score, it routes to three automated responses: a Slack alert plus AI re-confirmation email for super high risk, a friendly AI reminder email for high risk, and a silent log for low risk.

## Setup
1. Create a Google Sheets file with a tab named customer_history (columns: customer_email, customer_name, total_bookings, no_show_count, last_booking_date, last_status)
2. Open the Set Configuration node and fill in your Sheet ID and Slack channel
3. Connect Google Calendar, Google Sheets, Gmail, Slack, and OpenAI credentials
4. Activate the workflow

## Requirements
- Google Calendar with appointment bookings
- Google Sheets for customer history
- Slack workspace for alerts
- OpenAI API key (used only for generating email text)
- Gmail account with OAuth2 access

## How to customize
- Adjust the risk thresholds in Set Configuration (default: 70 for Super High, 40 for High)
- Edit the AI prompts in Generate Urgent Message and Generate Reminder nodes to match your tone
- Replace Slack with Discord webhook if preferred

## Important note
Risk scoring is 100% rule-based. AI is used only for generating reminder text, never for the risk judgment itself.

workflow.name: Predict No-show Risk and Route Reminders from Google Calendar Bookings

parameters.content:
## Predict No-show Risk and Route Reminders from Google Calendar Bookings

This workflow predicts appointment no-show risk using historical customer data and sends differentiated reminders based on risk level.

## How it works
1. Runs daily at 9 AM to check tomorrow's bookings
2. Fetches scheduled appointments from Google Calendar
3. Looks up each customer's history from Google Sheets
4. Calculates a risk score (0-100) from four weighted signals
5. Routes to three paths based on risk level

Super High (>=70): Slack alert + AI re-confirmation email
High (>=40): AI-generated friendly reminder email
Low (<40): Silent log for records

## Trigger & Configuration

Runs daily at 9 AM.
All settings managed in Set Configuration.

## Data Collection

Fetches tomorrow's bookings from Google Calendar.
Looks up each customer's history from Google Sheets.

## Risk Assessment

Calculates score (0-100)
from 4 weighted signals:
- No-show rate (40%)
- Lead time (20%)
- Day/time pattern (20%)
- Recent cancellations (20%)

## Risk Routing

Routes by score:
- Super High (>=70)
- High (>=40)
- Low (<40)

## Super High (>=70)

Slack alert + re-confirmation email.

## High (>=40)

AI reminder email to customer.

## Low (<40)

Silent log to Google Sheets.

values.content: You are a polite scheduling assistant. Write a brief re-confirmation email that asks the customer to confirm attendance. Warm, clear, under 5 sentences. Plain text only.

parameters.subject: =Please confirm your appointment on {{ $('Calculate Risk Score').item.json.start_time }}

values.content: You are a friendly scheduling assistant. Write a short reminder email in warm, casual tone. Under 4 sentences. Plain text only.

parameters.content:
## Setup steps
1. Create Google Sheets with customer_history tab
   (customer_email, customer_name, total_bookings, no_show_count, last_booking_date, last_status)
2. Open Set Configuration and fill in Sheet ID and Slack channel
3. Connect Google Calendar, Sheets, Gmail, Slack, and OpenAI credentials
4. Activate the workflow

Note: Risk scoring is 100% rule-based.
AI is used only for generating reminder text, never for the risk judgment itself.
